name: Is it safe to ask ChatGPT about my legal case before talking to a real lawyer?
description: No, AI chats aren't privileged and can be discovered in court—stick to real lawyers.[1]

mainEntity.name: Is it safe to ask ChatGPT about my legal case before talking to a real lawyer?

acceptedAnswer.text: No, AI chats aren't privileged and can be discovered in court—stick to real lawyers.[1]
acceptedAnswer.description: No, AI conversations are not protected by attorney-client privilege and can be used as evidence against you in court.[1] A 2026 New York federal court ruling in *United States v.* confirmed that AI like Claude isn't a lawyer, so prompts about your case are discoverable by opponents.[1] Key factors: AI lacks confidentiality, no attorney relationship forms, and inconsistencies in your AI chats can undermine your story.[1][3] Avoid inputting case details into AI; consult a licensed attorney immediately for protected advice. If you've already used AI, disclose it to your lawyer early.
